title: poa glaberrima
intro: Poa glaberrima, also known as rough meadow-grass or meadow oat-grass, is a species of grass native to Europe and Western Asia. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and soothing respiratory problems. Poa glaberrima is often prepared as a tea or infusion, with the dried leaves and flowers being steeped in hot water to create a herbal remedy.
